name: MARFAN SYNDROME
definition: Arachnodactyly autosomal dominant (in 75%) multisystemic connective tissue disorder with high penetrance ballooning / widening of dural sac ± nerve root sleeves

demographics.Genetics: > 135 mutations in fibrillin-1 gene (FBN1) on chromosome 15 which encodes a large
demographics.Path: disintegration and elastolysis of connective tissue with abnormal cross-linking of
demographics.Histo: degeneration of elastic tissue in aortic media + cystic medial necrosis of smooth

discriminating_features: increased attenuation of thrombosed false lumen

CXR: progressive aortic enlargement at serial imaging
CT: increased attenuation of thrombosed false lumen
infarction of organs supplied by branch vessels from the false lumen
Foot: pes planus (25%) / planovalgus ← increased ligamentous laxity
disproportionate elongation of 1st digit of foot
Chest wall deformity: pectus excavatum = retraction of lower sternum:
pectus carinatum = anterior protrusion of upper portion of sternum
Spine: severe rigid progressive scoliosis (45-62%) / kyphosis (16%) / kyphoscoliosis
increased incidence of Schmorl nodes (Scheuermann disease) and spondylolisthesis
Joints: recurrent dislocations of patella, hip, clavicle, mandible
progressive protrusio acetabuli (16-27%) = invasion of acetabulum + femoral head
"teardrop" crossed by ilioischial line / obscured by femoral head
Aorta (cause of death in 55%): "tulip bulb aorta" = symmetrical dilatation of aortic sinuses of Valsalva slightly
triangular coaptation defect of valve cusps (during middiastolic closure at 70% of
tethered valve cusps at midsystolic opening (= at 10% of R-R interval of a gated
fusiform dilatation of ascending aorta, rarely beyond innominate artery (rapid
more rapid enlargement than atherosclerotic aneurysm
rarely intimal calcifications of the aortic wall
Mitral valve: "floppy valve syndrome" (95%) = redundant chordae tendineae with mitral valve
Cor pulmonale ← chest deformity: lung cysts, bullae, blebs + diffuse apical bullous emphysema
recurrent spontaneous pneumothoraces (4-15%; 10 x times more common than in general

clinical.Cx: aortic regurgitation (in 81% if root diameter > 5 cm; in 100% if root diameter
clinical.Prognosis: average age of death at 35 (75) years in untreated (treated) patients
